title: elaeocarpus halconensis
intro: Elaeocarpus halconensis, also known as the Philippine blueberry or Philippine blue olive, is a plant species in the Elaeocarpaceae family. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is native to the Philippines and is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and fruits are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Elaeocarpus halconensis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and its leaves can also be used as a poultice or applied topically to wounds and skin conditions, providing relief and promoting healing.
